# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-slim

ENV NODE_ENV=production

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./

RUN npm ci --only=production && \
    npm cache clean --force

COPY . .

RUN useradd -r -u 1001 -g root appuser && \
    chown -R appuser:root /app

USER appuser

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "index.js"]
